summary: Decompiled most of melee/ft/ft_07C1.c, taking the file to 99.37% overall. Four functions, ft_8007C114, ft_8007C17C, ft_8007C224, and ft_8007C2E0, reached 100%; ft_8007C4BC remained at 97.80% with the only reported score loss. The work also improved fighter and collision typing around thrown hitboxes, ftData, HitCapsule, and fighter gobj types, then marked corresponding scratch entries as OK.
what_changed: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies in ft_8007C17C, ft_8007C224, ft_8007C2E0, and ft_8007C4BC with C implementations.

Implemented thrown-hitbox setup in ft_8007C17C using GET_FIGHTER, fp->x1064_thrownHitbox, fp->ft_data->x34, lbColl_80008440, part joint selection, scale setup, flag clearing, and a call to ft_8007C224.

Implemented ft_8007C224 as a HitCapsule state machine over HitCapsule_Disabled, HitCapsule_Enabled, HitCapsule_Unk2, and HitCapsule_Unk3, including the intentional fallthrough from Unk2 to Unk3 and lb_8000B1CC position updates.
Implemented ft_8007C2E0 as mirrored hit-collision/weight-transfer logic using lbColl_8000ACFC, lbColl_80008688, p_ftCommonData->hit_weight_mul, co_attrs.weight, pos_delta, and x98_atk_shield_kb.

Implemented ft_8007C4BC as a fighter-list traversal over HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters with filters for grab attackers, collision state, thrown-hitbox enabled state, and lbColl_80007AFC/lbColl_800089B8 collision handling.
Changed ft_07C1.h prototypes from generic HSD_GObj*/void placeholders to typed Fighter_GObj*, Fighter*, and HitCapsule* signatures.
Added ftData and ftData_x34 structure detail in src/melee/ft/types.h, including x8 byte fields x10 through x14 and x34 containing Fighter_Part x0 plus scale.
Renamed ftCommonData +0x7D4 from an int placeholder to float hit_weight_mul.
Renamed HitCapsule field +0x48 from int x48 to HSD_JObj* jobj in both fighter/lb type usage and call sites.
Added HitCapsule_Unk3 and HitCapsule_Max to the HitCapsuleState enum with STATIC_ASSERT(HitCapsule_Max == 3).
Updated many call sites from fp->ft_data->x8->unk10/unk11/unk12/unk13/unk14 to x10/x11/x12/x13/x14 after the ftData_x8 layout change.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt entries for ft_8007C114, ft_8007C17C, ft_8007C224, ft_8007C2E0, and ft_8007C4BC from percent statuses to OK.
Updated local sdata2 symbol names at 0x804D8320 and 0x804D8324 from ft_804D8320/ft_804D8324 to @221/@263.
Regenerated asm/melee/ft/ft_07C1.s address comments and labels from scratch-relative labels like .L_00000040 to linked-address labels like .L_8007C154.
smart_moves: Typed the API surface first: using Fighter_GObj*, Fighter*, and HitCapsule* exposed field access cleanly and reduced reliance on raw HSD_GObj placeholders.
Used struct refinements to explain repeated offsets instead of leaving magic offsets in the decomp, especially ftData->x34 for thrown-hitbox joint/scale data and HitCapsule->jobj at +0x48.
Modeled ft_8007C224 as a switch with fallthrough, matching the assembly state transition pattern while keeping readable control flow.

Kept duplicated inline-like logic explicit in ft_8007C2E0, with a source comment noting that it is the same inline twice; this is a useful matching tactic when extracting an inline would perturb codegen.
Used PAD_STACK(4 * 4) in ft_8007C2E0 to preserve the required stack shape.
Added HitCapsule_Max plus a static assert to lock down the enum value implied by the assembly comparisons.
Converted scratch records to OK after matching, preserving older scratch provenance while making current status searchable.
decomp_lessons: When a small file mostly consists of interactions among existing systems, matching may require improving neighboring headers and struct fields, not only writing the target C file.
A meaningful field rename such as HitCapsule.x48 to jobj can cascade through multiple files but makes later call signatures like lb_8000B1CC(hitbox->jobj, ...) much clearer.
For repeated mirrored collision code, duplicating inline bodies may be more match-friendly than prematurely factoring into a helper, even if a static inline sketch exists.
State-machine functions often match naturally as switch statements with intentional fallthrough; record the fallthrough by structure rather than by awkward gotos when codegen permits.
Use typed forward declarations, such as Fighter_GObj and HitCapsule, to avoid including heavier headers in public headers while still making prototypes precise.
Local float constants may need compiler-style @NNN labels in symbols.txt when matching generated local sdata2 references.
Updating scratch status to OK is part of the decomp workflow and helps future agents avoid redoing already matched scratch functions.
naming_conventions: Use Fighter_GObj* for fighter object parameters in fighter-specific functions instead of generic HSD_GObj* when the function immediately accesses Fighter data.
Use xNN names for unknown ftData byte fields, as in ftData_x8.x10 through x14, rather than unkNN when the structure is being normalized around offsets.
Promote placeholder fields to semantic names when evidence is strong: ftCommonData.x7D4 became hit_weight_mul and HitCapsule.x48 became jobj.
Use small layout helper names that include the parent field offset when semantics remain partial, such as ftData_x34 and struct ftData_x8.
Keep compiler-local float constants named in @NNN form when symbols.txt evidence shows local sdata2 references such as @221 and @263.
Retain address-based function names such as ft_8007C2E0 and ft_8007C4BC when no higher-level behavior name is established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched ft_8007C2E0 with explicit duplicated inline blocks instead of calling the static inline helper, matching the PR comment: '@todo This is just the same inline twice.'
Used PAD_STACK(4 * 4) to satisfy stack layout for ft_8007C2E0.
Preserved switch fallthrough from HitCapsule_Unk2 into HitCapsule_Unk3 in ft_8007C224 to match the branch layout.
Used float comparisons and clamp-to-1 logic around weight ratios to match collision knockback transfer code.
Used boolean bitfields such as x2219_b1, x2227_b2, x221F_b4, x221C_b6, and x43_b1/x43_b2 directly, avoiding raw bit masking in C.
The asm file was regenerated from scratch-relative offsets to linked addresses and absolute labels, showing the functions were integrated into the linked object context.
The remaining mismatch was isolated to ft_8007C4BC, reported as 372 bytes with score 205 out of 9300, or 97.80%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or rename ft_8007C4BC.
Search for additional uses of ftData_x34 to determine better names for x0 and scale.
Search for HitCapsule_Unk2 and HitCapsule_Unk3 to refine state names.
Search for p_ftCommonData->hit_weight_mul uses to confirm the +0x7D4 semantic name.
Check whether ftData_x8.x10 through x14 were later given semantic bone or item-part names.
